children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Any order issued under this chapter shall contain the terms and provisions specified in this section.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The order shall provide for the establishment of a Concrete Masonry Products Board to carry out a program of generic promotion, research, and education regarding concrete masonry products.

children.heading: Number of members
children.label: i Number of members
children.text: The Board shall consist of not fewer than 15 and not more than 25 members.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: ii Appointment
children.text: The members of the Board shall be appointed by the Secretary from nominations submitted as provided in the order.

children.heading: Composition
children.label: iii Composition
children.text: The Board shall consist of manufacturers. No employee of an industry trade organization exempt from tax under paragraph (3) or (6) of representing the concrete masonry industry or related industries shall serve as a member of the Board and no member of the Board may serve concurrently as an officer of the board of directors of a national concrete masonry products industry trade association. Only 2 individuals from any single company or its affiliates may serve on the Board at any one time.section 501(c) of title 26

children.heading: Representation
children.label: A Representation
children.text: To ensure fair and equitable representation of the concrete masonry products industry, the composition of the Board shall reflect the geographical distribution of the manufacture of concrete masonry products in the United States, the types of concrete masonry products manufactured, and the range in size of manufacturers in the United States.

children.heading: Adjustment in Board representation
children.label: B Adjustment in Board representation
children.text: Three years after the assessment of concrete masonry products commences pursuant to an order, and at the end of each 3-year period thereafter, the Board, subject to the review and approval of the Secretary, shall, if warranted, recommend to the Secretary the reapportionment of the Board membership to reflect changes in the geographical distribution of the manufacture of concrete masonry products and the types of concrete masonry products manufactured.

children.heading: Nominations process
children.label: 3 Nominations process
children.text: The Secretary may make appointments from nominations by manufacturers pursuant to the method set forth in the order.

children.heading: Failure to appoint
children.label: 4 Failure to appoint
children.text: If the Secretary fails to make an appointment to the Board within 60 days of receiving nominations for such appointment, the first nominee for such appointment shall be deemed appointed, unless the Secretary provides reasonable justification for the delay to the Board and to Congress and provides a reasonable date by which approval or disapproval will be made.

children.heading: Alternates
children.label: 5 Alternates
children.text: The order shall provide for the selection of alternate members of the Board by the Secretary in accordance with procedures specified in the order.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The members and any alternates of the Board shall each serve for a term of 3 years, except that members and any alternates initially appointed to the Board shall serve for terms of not more than 2, 3, and 4 years, as specified by the order.

children.heading: Limitation on consecutive terms
children.label: B Limitation on consecutive terms
children.text: A member or an alternate may serve not more than 2 consecutive terms.

children.heading: Continuation of term
children.label: C Continuation of term
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (B), each member or alternate shall continue to serve until a successor is appointed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: D Vacancies
children.text: A vacancy arising before the expiration of a term of office of an incumbent member or alternate of the Board shall be filled in a manner provided for in the order.

children.heading: Disqualification from Board service
children.label: 7 Disqualification from Board service
children.text: The order shall provide that if a member or alternate of the Board who was appointed as a manufacturer ceases to qualify as a manufacturer, such member or alternate shall be disqualified from serving on the Board.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Members and any alternates of the Board shall serve without compensation.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: B Travel expenses
children.text: If approved by the Board, members or alternates shall be reimbursed for reasonable travel expenses, which may include per diem allowance or actual subsistence incurred while away from their homes or regular places of business in the performance of services for the Board.

children.heading: Powers and duties of the Board
children.label: c Powers and duties of the Board

children.text: to administer the order in accordance with its terms and conditions and to collect assessments;

to develop and recommend to the Secretary for approval such bylaws as may be necessary for the functioning of the Board and such rules as may be necessary to administer the order, including activities authorized to be carried out under the order;
to meet, organize, and select from among members of the Board a chairperson, other officers, and committees and subcommittees, as the Board determines appropriate;
to establish regional organizations or committees to administer regional initiatives;
to establish working committees of persons other than Board members;
to employ such persons, other than the members, as the Board considers necessary, and to determine the compensation and specify the duties of the persons;

to prepare and submit for the approval of the Secretary, before the beginning of each fiscal year, rates of assessment under and an annual budget of the anticipated expenses to be incurred in the administration of the order, including the probable cost of each promotion, research, and information activity proposed to be developed or carried out by the Board;section 8705 of this title
to borrow funds necessary for the startup expenses of the order;

to carry out generic research, education, and promotion programs and projects relating to concrete masonry products, and to pay the costs of such programs and projects with assessments collected under ;section 8705 of this title
subject to subsection (e), to enter into contracts or agreements to develop and carry out programs or projects of research, education, and promotion relating to concrete masonry products;
to keep minutes, books, and records that reflect the actions and transactions of the Board, and promptly report minutes of each Board meeting to the Secretary;
to receive, investigate, and report to the Secretary complaints of violations of the order;
to furnish the Secretary with such information as the Secretary may request;
to recommend to the Secretary such amendments to the order as the Board considers appropriate; and
to provide the Secretary with advance notice of meetings to permit the Secretary, or the representative of the Secretary, to attend the meetings.

children.heading: Programs and projects; budgets; expenses
children.label: d Programs and projects; budgets; expenses

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The order shall require the Board to submit to the Secretary for approval any program or project of research, education, or promotion relating to concrete masonry products.

children.heading: Statement required
children.label: B Statement required
children.text: Any educational or promotional activity undertaken with funds provided by the Board shall include a statement that such activities were supported in whole or in part by the Board.

children.heading: Submission
children.label: A Submission
children.text: The order shall require the Board to submit to the Secretary for approval a budget of the anticipated expenses and disbursements of the Board in the implementation of the order, including the projected costs of concrete masonry products research, education, and promotion programs and projects.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: B Timing
children.text: The budget shall be submitted before the beginning of a fiscal year and as frequently as may be necessary after the beginning of the fiscal year.

children.heading: Approval
children.label: C Approval
children.text: If the Secretary fails to approve or reject a budget within 60 days of receipt, such budget shall be deemed approved, unless the Secretary provides to the Board and to Congress, in writing, reasonable justification for the delay and provides a reasonable date by which approval or disapproval will be made.

children.heading: Incurring expenses
children.label: A Incurring expenses
children.text: The Board may incur the expenses described in paragraph (2) and other expenses for the administration, maintenance, and functioning of the Board as authorized by the Secretary.

children.heading: Payment of expenses
children.label: B Payment of expenses
children.text: Expenses incurred under subparagraph (A) shall be paid by the Board using assessments collected under , earnings obtained from assessments, and other income of the Board. Any funds borrowed by the Board shall be expended only for startup costs and capital outlays.section 8705 of this title

children.heading: Limitation on spending
children.label: C Limitation on spending
children.text: For fiscal years beginning 3 or more years after the date of the establishment of the Board, the Board may not expend for administration (except for reimbursement to the Secretary required under subparagraph (D)), maintenance, and functioning of the Board in a fiscal year an amount that exceeds 10 percent of the assessment and other income received by the Board for the fiscal year.

children.heading: Reimbursement of Secretary
children.label: D Reimbursement of Secretary
children.text: The order shall require that the Secretary be reimbursed by the Board from assessments for all expenses incurred by the Secretary in the implementation, administration, and supervision of the order, including all referenda costs incurred in connection with the order.

children.label: A
children.text: enter into contracts and agreements to carry out generic research, education, and promotion programs and projects relating to concrete masonry products, including contracts and agreements with manufacturer associations or other entities as considered appropriate by the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: enter into contracts and agreements for administrative services; and

children.label: C
children.text: pay the cost of approved generic research, education, and promotion programs and projects using assessments collected under , earnings obtained from assessments, and other income of the Board.section 8705 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: develop and submit to the Board a proposed program or project together with a budget that specifies the cost to be incurred to carry out the program or project;

children.label: B
children.text: keep accurate records of all transactions relating to the contract or agreement;

children.label: C
children.text: account for funds received and expended in connection with the contract or agreement;

children.label: D
children.text: make periodic reports to the Board of activities conducted under the contract or agreement; and

children.label: E
children.text: make such other reports as the Board or the Secretary considers relevant.

children.heading: Failure to approve
children.label: 3 Failure to approve
children.text: If the Secretary fails to approve or reject a contract or agreement entered into under paragraph (1) within 60 days of receipt, the contract or agreement shall be deemed approved, unless the Secretary provides to the Board and to Congress, in writing, reasonable justification for the delay and provides a reasonable date by which approval or disapproval will be made.

children.heading: Books and records of Board
children.label: f Books and records of Board
A
children.text: maintain such books and records (which shall be available to the Secretary for inspection and audit) as the Secretary may require;

children.label: B
children.text: collect and submit to the Secretary, at any time the Secretary may specify, any information the Secretary may request; and

children.label: C
children.text: account for the receipt and disbursement of all funds in the possession, or under the control, of the Board.

children.label: A
children.text: the books and records of the Board audited by an independent auditor at the end of each fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: a report of the audit submitted directly to the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: influence legislation, elections, or governmental action;

children.label: B
children.text: engage in an action that would be a conflict of interest;

children.label: C
children.text: engage in advertising that is false or misleading;

children.label: D
children.text: engage in any promotion, research, or education that would be disparaging to other construction materials; or

children.label: E
children.text: engage in any promotion or project that would benefit any individual manufacturer.

children.label: A
children.text: the development and recommendation of amendments to the order;

children.label: B
children.text: the communication to appropriate government officials of information relating to the conduct, implementation, or results of research, education, and promotion activities under the order except communications described in paragraph (1)(A); or

children.label: C
children.text: any lawful action designed to market concrete masonry products directly to a foreign government or political subdivision of a foreign government.

children.heading: Periodic evaluation
children.label: h Periodic evaluation
children.text: The order shall require the Board to provide for the independent evaluation of all research, education, and promotion programs or projects undertaken under the order, beginning 5 years after , and every 3 years thereafter. The Board shall submit to the Secretary and make available to the public the results of each such evaluation.October 5, 2018

children.heading: Objectives
children.label: i Objectives
children.text: The Board shall establish annual research, education, and promotion objectives and performance metrics for each fiscal year subject to approval by the Secretary.
the overall financial condition of the Board;
a summary of the amounts obligated or expended during the 2 preceding fiscal years; and
a description of the extent to which the objectives of the Board were met according to the metrics required under subsection (i).

children.heading: Books and records of persons covered by order
children.label: k Books and records of persons covered by order
A
children.text: maintain records sufficient to ensure compliance with the order and regulations; and

children.label: B
children.text: make the records described in subparagraph (A) available, during normal business hours, for inspection by employees or agents of the Board or the Department.

children.heading: Time requirement
children.label: 2 Time requirement
children.text: Any record required to be maintained under paragraph (1) shall be maintained for such time period as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, trade secrets and commercial or financial information that is privileged or confidential reported to, or otherwise obtained by the Board or the Secretary (or any representative of the Board or the Secretary) under this chapter shall not be disclosed by any officers, employees, and agents of the Department or the Board.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary considers the information relevant; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the information is revealed in a judicial proceeding or administrative hearing brought at the direction or on the request of the Secretary or to which the Secretary or any officer of the Department is a party.

children.label: i
children.text: the issuance of general statements based on reports or on information relating to a number of persons subject to an order if the statements do not identify the information furnished by any person; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the publication, by direction of the Secretary, of the name of any person violating any order and a statement of the particular provisions of the order violated by the person.

children.heading: Penalty
children.label: D Penalty
children.text: Any officer, employee, or agent of the Department of Commerce or any officer, employee, or agent of the Board who willfully violates this paragraph shall be fined not more than $1,000 and imprisoned for not more than 1 year, or both.

children.heading: Withholding information
children.label: 4 Withholding information
children.text: This subsection does not authorize the withholding of information from Congress.
